title: Is Mari British or American
wikitext:
[]: Is Mari British or American? Post by Rahaquiel-2D1S » Sat Nov 21, 2009 1:57 pm I mean, I heard the Illustrious was a British battleship, so she'd be British if we're following the ship-naming theme. But I've heard some people say she's American. Personally, I'm inclined to believe she's British. SPOILER: Show

[]: Post by NAveryW » Sat Nov 21, 2009 2:07 pm She's British.

[]: Post by Jesterhead » Sat Nov 21, 2009 2:09 pm I get the feeling that Mari is European, but not British. Listening to the way she talked on her cell phone reminded me of the way someone from Europe would talk. Somehow it just didn't feel like someone speaking english with a Japanese accent to me.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Nov 21, 2009 2:09 pm English speaking and European. Well, she could be a bog-rat, I guess. Otherwise, do the math.

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Nov 21, 2009 2:20 pm She's from space.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Nov 21, 2009 2:34 pm She's British. SPOILER: Show

[]: Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sat Nov 21, 2009 3:07 pm She's Ethiopian. That or she's Australian cause they love danger Bruce!

[]: Post by SaltyJoe » Sat Nov 21, 2009 4:30 pm Going by this interview with Sadamoto, the intention was to amke her look British. As for her first name, it's more like Mary of Bethany. Or Mary Sue. But who's Jesus, then?

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Nov 21, 2009 4:36 pm Shinji. Or Rei. Or if we're going by the Mari/Kaworu twins rumor-mill then Kaworu.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Sat Nov 21, 2009 4:45 pm SaltyJoe wrote: But who's Jesus, then?

[]: Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sat Nov 21, 2009 5:05 pm In b4 lock... KAWORU!
